content: Thyroid cancer is the most common endocrine malignancy. Four main histologic types: (1) Papillary thyroid carcinoma (PTC) - 80-85% of cases, arises from follicular cells, often spreads via lymphatics, excellent prognosis; (2) Follicular thyroid carcinoma (FTC) - 10-15%, hematogenous spread to bone/lung, prognosis intermediate; (3) Medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC) - 3-5%, from parafollicular C-cells, secretes calcitonin, 25% hereditary (MEN2 syndromes); (4) Anaplastic thyroid carcinoma (ATC) - 1-2%, undifferentiated, aggressive with poor prognosis (median survival months). Risk factors: radiation exposure (especially childhood), family history, iodine deficiency (follicular), genetic mutations (BRAF V600E in PTC, RET in MTC). Incidence increasing due to detection of small papillary cancers.
